raw_text:
Do not, under any circumstances, open an account with Marcus… In March of 2025, I opened an account with Marcus.
After no more than a weeks time, my account was locked, and soon after that, my account was closed completely. They say it’s because I was in violation of one of their policies, and that is their right, and I will not waste time arguing the minutia of what they saw versus what actually happened, but I’m not happy with the fact that they never told me which policy was violated or how I violated the policy. I never got a warning that they were going to close or lock the account. I woke up one morning, logged onto the account, only to find the account was frozen.
As of now, they still have the money that I deposited to open the account in the first place. I’ve placed numerous calls to them, asking/demanding/complaining for my money to be returned to me and as of this writing, I still don’t have my money back. They keep insisting that my money is “perfectly safe with them”. My opinion is that the money isn’t perfectly safe with them because it’s not perfectly safe with me.
To me, this almost seems like theft. Locked from Account and no way to Verify On August 16, I called because my account was locked. They asked for my phone number and told me to wait until August 20. I called again on August 20, and they asked for my phone number and my wife's phone number. They also asked for our driver's licenses, and when they realized the licenses were from California, they couldn’t verify them. I was told to wait 3-4 business days.

When I called on August 23, I was informed that the representatives couldn’t send the one-time password because neither my wife’s nor my phone number was secure enough. They also asked for my license again. I was told that I would need to visit a notary to verify my identity and that I should wait 4-7 business days for the document to be sent.

I called back on August 30, and they started asking for my phone number and license again. I told them that neither was working and that I was calling to confirm that the document was being sent. They ignored me and insisted I provide my phone number again, only to find out it was still not secure. They said I would receive the document to bring to the notary within 7-10 business days from that day. When I spoke to Supervisor Bobby, he told me that the previous 4-7 day estimate was incorrect.

DO NOT USE MARCUS TERRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE! I CASE IS NOT THE ONLY CASE OF INDIVIDUALS GETTING PERMANENTLY LOCKED FROM ACCOUNT.

raw_text: This bank is a literal SCAM TLDR: My experience with Marcus savings has truly been the epitome of shady, incompetent online banking. I'm not someone to write bad reviews, but this has been such a frustrating and ridiculous situation, and I want to warn everyone, please, DO NOT trust this bank. It's one thing if an account is closed and customer service isn't able to provide an explanation behind this, but shouldn't getting the funds back be made an absolute priority? I'm not sure why this bank has deliberately made this become such a complicated and stressful process, where even after 4 YEARS they still have refused to give my money back. I had an account opened with Marcus back in 2020, which suddenly got closed a few months later. When I tried calling customer service, they refused to give me any explanation behind the account's closure. After several attempts to call them regarding getting the money back into my original bank account, I still never received my funds. In July, I had a family emergency that required me to travel for a few weeks - prior to leaving, I desperately tried to call back to see if my money could be sent back to me before I left. They said that a check would be mailed to me within 14 business days and to my surprise, there was no check in the mail once I got back. Again, I tried to call them regarding this, and they stated that their so-called 'review team' would be reopening my account within 30 business days instead. I called again today (which is when I should have received an update regarding the reopening of my account) and yet again, still there is no update regarding if or when I'll be able to get my money back. Endless attempts to speak with both customer service and specialists have been failures, as they are entirely clueless about any updates from the 'review team', and now I haven't been given any timeframe as to when I'll be receiving an update at all. It's rather repulsing to see that a bank with such a good reputation and reviews, being "rated #1 in customer service" can be this negligent and incapable of addressing basic matters.

Used to be Great, not so much anymore When I first started using this app, it allowed you to check your net worth by connecting all of your accounts to Marcus. That feature was removed and the app became much simpler and not really that insightful.

I still kept my money in there because the Marcus preferred the program gave you an extra 1% APY (5.4% total!) on your savings account for three months per person that you referred. raw_text:
HATE the new credit card payment process … Went to make a payment on husband’s card, and found that the linked bank account we make payments from had been deleted, not by either of us, we aren’t sure how or why (we have been making payments from that account for years and even made a payment from that account a couple days before without issues). Re-linked the same exact bank account, but now the payment process is totally different. Now we have to select which bank to pay from (there’s only one), then select between the statement balance or custom payment, and select the date to make the payment on. These are all on separate pages with too many button clicks. After that, the screen goes immediately to the payment activity page instead of back to the main credit card page I went into the payment page from that shows all transactions on the card and the balance. 

When I go into my card to make a payment, everything is on one page, with the normal bank account defaulted as the selected option, and the statement balance or enter custom amount option available to choose from, and the current date selected as the default already, with a link to change if desired (which I never change). This process is WAY smoother and preferred across all credit cards as the payment process. 

I do like that when the payments are entered, the pending payment and updated balance after the payment will be made shows immediately on the overall credit card transaction page, but wish that was the only change instead of the overall payment process. The new process has WAY too many button clicks and is very time consuming and not ideal for the end user in the slightest.

raw_text:
Locked me out of my account I opened a new account, funded it, received a direct deposit, and attempted to send money with Zelle. Rather than just denying my transaction, it locked me out of my account, so now I can’t even see my money, much less use it.

I called their support line, which had rather poor quality phone technology which made it hard to understand the person on the other end, and verified my identity in multiple ways as they specified. After all of this, the individual told me that it didn’t matter that they had verified my identity because I still wouldn’t be able to access my account until I receive my card, why could be in over a week.

It is bewildering to me why you would not make this known to a customer. Why would you allow a customer to set up Zelle, verify their identity, and then be locked out of their account with no way to access it until they receive their debit card, assuming no delivery complications? This is a horrible and unfair policy. Why would you not simply prohibit Zelle set up until they receive their debit card?

I am extremely disappointed with this app and probably will not bank with Citi for much longer after this experience.

raw_text:
Unreliable, not user friendly The Citi Mobile app is one of the most frustrating banking experiences I’ve had. The interface is unintuitive and error-prone in ways that have real financial consequences.
I initiated a transfer FROM my account, and the app processed it as a transfer TO my account, the exact opposite of what I commanded. This wasn’t a minor inconvenience. The mistake overdrafted my other account, causing fees and financial stress that should never result from a simple transfer.
When a banking app can’t correctly process the direction of a fund transfer, it isn’t just a UX problem. It’s a liability. I expect basic reliability from a financial institution’s app. Citi Mobile failed that standard completely. I’ll be managing my transactions through a branch or a more dependable platform until this is fixed.

raw_text: Predatory This is probably the most predatory credit company app I’ve used. The way they post your total is confusing. Payments post immediately but aren’t always reflected in the pending balance. The posted totals never match your balance. Most companies will have a way of showing the running total in your transactions but Citi does not, so your posted total will always lag behind by over a week, leading to confusion about whether the balance matches your spending or not. Payments will post to your pending balance showing negative and then your actual balance won’t match that. Other companies I’ve used will compensate for this posted total vs running total discrepancy but Citi does not, so good luck knowing how much is due. I’ve paid too much several times to keep up with the balance, paid too little others. The only way I can compensate is by keeping a separate log. In which case what is the actual point of using this to help track spending? I am shopping for a new credit card and plan on lodging a complaint with Cosco to make sure they’re aware how bad this company is. A shame that Cosco is associated with this garbage. Hopefully they’ll pick a new company with better spending tracking so that I can use their card again.

Extremely Frustrating and Unprofessional Customer Service I recently received a replacement Citi Costco Visa card. raw_text:
Find another credit card company I’ve had a really disappointing experience. I never received any alerts about a payment issue until two days after it failed — and it was barely over $100. Then, three days later, I find out the payment was returned with no notice and I was charged both a return fee and an interest fee.

I check my cards daily, and there were no app notifications or emails until five days past the due date saying my payment was late. That’s unacceptable. To make things right, I paid off the full balance immediately — over $1,300 — yet when I contacted support to request a courtesy waiver for the fees, I was told they couldn’t do anything.
